title: nothing
description: testing one

title: Completion of the Large Hadron Collider (LHC)
description: The Large Hadron Collider, the world's largest and highest-energy particle accelerator, was completed by CERN in collaboration with over 10,000 scientists worldwide.

title: James Webb Space Telescope Launch
description: Webb launched on December 25, orbiting the Sun at the second Lagrange point (L2) to study every phase of cosmic history, from the Big Bang to the evolution of the Solar System.

title: Aristotle's Contributions to Science
description: Aristotle wrote widely on scientific subjects and laid the foundations for much of the work that followed, though he relied entirely on thought and argument without conducting experiments.

title: Galileo Disproves Aristotle's Falling Objects Theory
description: Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei disproved Aristotle's assertion that heavier objects fall faster than lighter ones, introducing the importance of empirical evidence.

title: Work of Alhazen on Scientific Method
description: Arab scientist Alhazen laid foundational concepts for the scientific method approximately 600 years before Francis Bacon.

title: Francis Bacon's Scientific Method
description: English philosopher Francis Bacon introduced a systematic method for scientific processes that emphasized observation, hypothesis formation, experimentation, and peer review.

title: notinh
description: notinh

title: Thales Predicts Solar Eclipse
description: Thales of Miletus successfully predicted an eclipse of the Sun, marking an early intersection of science and philosophy.

title: Pythagoras Establishes Mathematical School
description: Pythagoras founded a school dedicated to mathematics and philosophy in what is now southern Italy, profoundly influencing both disciplines.

title: Xenophanes Theorizes Earth's Seafaring Past
description: After observing seashells on a mountain, Xenophanes deduced that the Earth was once entirely covered by sea.

title: Empedocles Identifies Four Fundamental Elements
description: Empedocles theorized that earth, air, fire, and water were the primary components of all matter, influencing future scientific thought.

title: Halley's Comet Prediction
description: Edmond Halley predicted the return of the comet observed in 1682, correctly forecasting its reappearance in 1758, which confirmed its periodic orbit around the Sun.

title: Discovery of Atomic Nucleus
description: Ernest Rutherford and his students conducted the gold foil experiment, which revealed that alpha particles occasionally bounced back, leading to the discovery of the atomic nucleus.

title: Invention of the Battery
description: The invention of the battery laid the foundation for future discoveries in electromagnetism and electrochemistry.

title: Discovery of Cathode Rays
description: This discovery was critical for the eventual identification of the electron and understanding of atomic structure.

title: Discovery of the Electron
description: The identification of the electron provided a pivotal understanding of atomic structure, leading to modern atomic theory.

title: Work on Atomic Structure by Niels Bohr
description: Niels Bohr contributed significantly to understanding atomic structure and quantum theory during this period.

title: Prediction of the Higgs Particle
description: Theoretical physicists predicted the Higgs particle, a major milestone in particle physics, essential for the Standard Model.

title: First Recorded Observation of Halley's Comet
description: Chinese astronomers made the first recorded observation of Halley's Comet, marking an early contribution to the study of celestial phenomena.

title: Observation of Supernova (Crab Nebula)
description: Chinese astronomers documented a supernova explosion that is now identified as the Crab Nebula, adding valuable historical data to the field of astronomy.

title: Establishment of the House of Wisdom
description: The Abbasid caliphate founded the House of Wisdom in Baghdad, serving as a hub for advances in science, technology, and the collection and translation of Greek and Indian knowledge into Arabic.

title: Invention of the Astrolabe
description: Ingenious mechanical devices, including the astrolabe, were developed at the House of Wisdom. The astrolabe was a navigational tool that utilized the positions of stars.

title: Development of Alchemy and Distillation Techniques
description: Alchemy flourished, and techniques such as distillation were pioneered during this period at the House of Wisdom.

title: Translation of Ancient Texts into Arabic
description: Scholars at the House of Wisdom translated important scientific and mathematical texts from Greece and India into Arabic, preserving ancient knowledge and introducing 'Arabic' numerals, including zero, into the Islamic world.
